codeReview.summary: The feature substantially delivers the requested 24 additional tool-calling problems and supporting tests, bringing the set to 40. However, one format-compliance checker is semantically incorrect and allows invalid outputs to pass.

issues.description: The fc_null_argument checker accepts empty-string or omitted bio values (`bio_val == ""` or missing key) even though this problem is specifically for null-argument compliance. This weakens validation and can mark non-null outputs as correct.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The python-worker skill mandates test-first (TDD), but the transcript shows implementation edits were made before tests were added/updated; handoff skillFeedback still reports followedProcedure=true.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Skill Step 3 in .factory/skills/python-worker/SKILL.md requires writing failing tests first; transcript skeleton for workerSessionId e0cdddf0-c29d-4a9e-aaa0-ed1c236d9e26 shows large implementation edits in tool_calling_problems.py before subsequent test-file updates.

summary: Reviewed feature tool-calling-problems-multi-edge-format at commit ee20a6f with transcript and diff evidence. Scope and counts are largely met, but the null-argument format-compliance check is too permissive and is marked as a blocking correctness issue.
